metadata.title: Med-X REVIEW Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases
authors: Yu Yong · Yicong Cai, · Jiawei Lin · Lin Ma · HongBin Han · Fenfang Li
metadata.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf

claims.text: Cells in the brain are surrounded by extracellular space (ECS), which forms porous nets and interconnected routes for molecule transportation.
claims.raw_text: Cells in the brain are surrounded by extracellular space (ECS), which forms porous nets and interconnected routes for molecule transportation.
support_evidence_snippet: Cells in the brain are surrounded by extracellular space (ECS), which forms porous nets and interconnected routes for molecule transportation.

claims.text: This review emphasizes modulation methods to manipulate ECS with implications to restore their function in brain diseases.
claims.raw_text: This review emphasizes modulation methods to manipulate ECS with implications to restore their function in brain diseases.
support_evidence_snippet: This review emphasizes modulation methods to manipulate ECS with implications to restore their function in brain diseases.

claims.text: • Advanced pharmacological and physical methods to manipulate brain ECS, which provides therapeutic potentials for treating neurological disorders.
claims.raw_text: • Advanced pharmacological and physical methods to manipulate brain ECS, which provides therapeutic potentials for treating neurological disorders.
support_evidence_snippet: • Advanced pharmacological and physical methods to manipulate brain ECS, which provides therapeutic potentials for treating neurological disorders.

claims.text: [page 2] Med-X (2024) 2:6 1 3 6 Page 2 of 33 Keywords Brain extracellular space · Regulation · Brain dysfunction · Advanced tools · Modulation · Neurological diseases
claims.raw_text: [page 2] Med-X (2024) 2:6 1 3 6 Page 2 of 33 Keywords Brain extracellular space · Regulation · Brain dysfunction · Advanced tools · Modulation · Neurological diseases
support_evidence_snippet: [page 2] Med-X (2024) 2:6 1 3 6 Page 2 of 33 Keywords Brain extracellular space · Regulation · Brain dysfunction · Advanced tools · Modulation · Neurological diseases

claims.text: Recent studies have revealed that the brain extracellular space (ECS) is a complex dynamic compartment that occupies approximately one-fifth of brain volume, filled with interstitial fluid (ISF), extracellular matrix (ECM), and other secreted molecules, actively involved in the regulation of molecule transportation, cell communications, and signal integration [2 –4].
claims.raw_text: Recent studies have revealed that the brain extracellular space (ECS) is a complex dynamic compartment that occupies approximately one-fifth of brain volume, filled with interstitial fluid (ISF), extracellular matrix (ECM), and other secreted molecules, actively involved in the regulation of molecule transportation, cell communications, and signal integration [2 –4].
support_evidence_snippet: Recent studies have revealed that the brain extracellular space (ECS) is a complex dynamic compartment that occupies approximately one-fifth of brain volume, filled with interstitial fluid (ISF), extracellular matrix (ECM), and other secreted molecules, actively involved in the regulation of molecule transportation, cell communications, and signal integration [2 –4].

claims.text: It is therefore important to investigate the structures, functions, and regulation of brain ECS in both normal and pathological conditions, which would facilitate our understanding of the brain homeostasis and disorders.
claims.raw_text: It is therefore important to investigate the structures, functions, and regulation of brain ECS in both normal and pathological conditions, which would facilitate our understanding of the brain homeostasis and disorders.
support_evidence_snippet: It is therefore important to investigate the structures, functions, and regulation of brain ECS in both normal and pathological conditions, which would facilitate our understanding of the brain homeostasis and disorders.

claims.text: Diffusion measurements were used to estimate the volume fraction (⍺ = ECS volume/total brain volume, normally 0.2–0.25) and the tortuosity (λ, normally 1.5–1.6), which indicates the hindrance imposed by brain ECS to molecular diffusion.
claims.raw_text: Diffusion measurements were used to estimate the volume fraction (⍺ = ECS volume/total brain volume, normally 0.2–0.25) and the tortuosity (λ, normally 1.5–1.6), which indicates the hindrance imposed by brain ECS to molecular diffusion.
support_evidence_snippet: Diffusion measurements were used to estimate the volume fraction (⍺ = ECS volume/total brain volume, normally 0.2–0.25) and the tortuosity (λ, normally 1.5–1.6), which indicates the hindrance imposed by brain ECS to molecular diffusion.

claims.text: At small scale, interaction of neurons and glia cannot be achieved without the involvement of the diffusion of transmitters and ions in the synaptic cleft via the nearby ECS.
claims.raw_text: At small scale, interaction of neurons and glia cannot be achieved without the involvement of the diffusion of transmitters and ions in the synaptic cleft via the nearby ECS.
support_evidence_snippet: At small scale, interaction of neurons and glia cannot be achieved without the involvement of the diffusion of transmitters and ions in the synaptic cleft via the nearby ECS.

claims.text: At large scale, the extensive communications between ISF in brain ECS and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) are important for the long-distance molecule transport, waste removal and homeostasis of brain microenvironment [13, 14].
claims.raw_text: At large scale, the extensive communications between ISF in brain ECS and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) are important for the long-distance molecule transport, waste removal and homeostasis of brain microenvironment [13, 14].
support_evidence_snippet: At large scale, the extensive communications between ISF in brain ECS and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) are important for the long-distance molecule transport, waste removal and homeostasis of brain microenvironment [13, 14].

claims.text: The dysregulation of ECS due to abnormal changes of ECM components or obstruction of ISF solutes transportation, has been closely associated with pathogenesis and progression of many neurological diseases, including Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, stroke, epilepsy and glioma [15– 17].
claims.raw_text: The dysregulation of ECS due to abnormal changes of ECM components or obstruction of ISF solutes transportation, has been closely associated with pathogenesis and progression of many neurological diseases, including Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, stroke, epilepsy and glioma [15– 17].
support_evidence_snippet: The dysregulation of ECS due to abnormal changes of ECM components or obstruction of ISF solutes transportation, has been closely associated with pathogenesis and progression of many neurological diseases, including Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, stroke, epilepsy and glioma [15– 17].

claims.text: Finally, the potential of using brain ECS knowledge in aging, bloodbrain barrier (BBB) integrity, extracellular vesicle traffick ing, drug delivery and space exploration are addressed.
claims.raw_text: Finally, the potential of using brain ECS knowledge in aging, bloodbrain barrier (BBB) integrity, extracellular vesicle traffick ing, drug delivery and space exploration are addressed.
support_evidence_snippet: Finally, the potential of using brain ECS knowledge in aging, bloodbrain barrier (BBB) integrity, extracellular vesicle traffick ing, drug delivery and space exploration are addressed.

claims.text: Regulation of ECS in healthy brain Brain ECS and neuronal activity The brain ECS is a reservoir for ions and neurotransmitters that are important for maintaining neural signaling transduction.

support_evidence_snippet: Regulation of ECS in healthy brain Brain ECS and neuronal activity The brain ECS is a reservoir for ions and neurotransmitters that are important for maintaining neural signaling transduction.

evidence.quote: These studies suggest that TMS promotes glym phatic Aβ clearance potentially through AQP4 repolarization. Beside the AD model, recent study also reported that rTMS treatment improved glymphatic function and molecule clearance in intracerebral hemorrhage model [255]. Interestingly, continuous theta burst TMS was found to modulate ISF penetration by activating GABAA receptors [256]. Whether TMS directly act on perivascular astrocyte AQP4 via GABA-dependent pathway remains to be explored.

evidence.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
evidence.quote: It is worth noting that the skull structure made it difficult for red light and near infrared light to penetrate. Although the longer wavelength of light increased the efficiency of transmission, the heating effects should not be ignored [217]. Intermittent stimulation instead of continuous treatment has been applied to reduce thermal effects, leading to positive effect on Aβ clearance [270]. Regardless, delivering sufficient light dose to deeper structures in the human brain with low heating effects continues to be challenging. Ultrasound stimulation A sound wave with a frequency higher than 20 kHz is called ultrasound. Ultrasound has been validated in clinical applications, particularly as an imaging tool for clinical diagnosis. Recently, ultrasound has emerged as a therapeutic tool for local drug or nanoparticle delivery by enlarging the inter stitial spaces within the tissue through non-thermal mechanisms [273–275]. For example, recent study demonstrated that the ECS and the perivascular space were enlarged in rat brain neocortical slices after exposed to low intensity ultrasound, promoting the diffusion of small diameter

figures.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
figures.caption: Under the electromagnetic field, the spin–lattice relaxation time of the hydrogen nucleus of H2 17O was shortened to produce high signal on MRI, which reveals the distribution of water in brain tissue in real time [216]. As the water diffuses, the signal decreases, while the distribution area increases over

figures.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
figures.caption: These studies suggest that TMS promotes glym phatic Aβ clearance potentially through AQP4 repolarization. Beside the AD model, recent study also reported that rTMS treatment improved glymphatic function and molecule clearance in intracerebral hemorrhage model [255]. Interestingly, continuous theta burst TMS was found to modulate ISF penetration by activating GABAA receptors [256]. Whether TMS directly act on perivascular astrocyte AQP4 via GABA-dependent pathway remains to be explored.

figures.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
figures.caption: It is worth noting that the skull structure made it difficult for red light and near infrared light to penetrate. Although the longer wavelength of light increased the efficiency of transmission, the heating effects should not be ignored [217]. Intermittent stimulation instead of continuous treatment has been applied to reduce thermal effects, leading to positive effect on Aβ clearance [270]. Regardless, delivering sufficient light dose to deeper structures in the human brain with low heating effects continues to be challenging. Ultrasound stimulation A sound wave with a frequency higher than 20 kHz is called ultrasound. Ultrasound has been validated in clinical applications, particularly as an imaging tool for clinical diagnosis. Recently, ultrasound has emerged as a therapeutic tool for local drug or nanoparticle delivery by enlarging the inter stitial spaces within the tissue through non-thermal mechanisms [273–275]. For example, recent study demonstrated that the ECS and the perivascular space were enlarged in rat brain neocortical slices after exposed to low intensity ultrasound, promoting the diffusion of small diameter

figures.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
figures.caption: It is also important to note that a closed-loop system for brain ECS manipulation that combines the stimulation feedback and real-time tracking of brain ECS dynamics is required for future exploration and therapeutic application. [page 18] Med-X (2024) 2:6 1 3 6 Page 18 of 33

provenance.model_infer_policy: Any pipeline-generated protocol, runbook, eval, and limitation text is marked model_infer and is not treated as source evidence.
provenance.claim_extractor_policy: Claims are raw paper excerpts only. raw_text and support_evidence_snippet are not paraphrased.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: Cells in the brain are surrounded by extracellular space (ECS), which forms porous nets and interconnected routes for molecule transportation.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: This review emphasizes modulation methods to manipulate ECS with implications to restore their function in brain diseases.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: • Advanced pharmacological and physical methods to manipulate brain ECS, which provides therapeutic potentials for treating neurological disorders.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: [page 2] Med-X (2024) 2:6 1 3 6 Page 2 of 33 Keywords Brain extracellular space · Regulation · Brain dysfunction · Advanced tools · Modulation · Neurological diseases

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: Recent studies have revealed that the brain extracellular space (ECS) is a complex dynamic compartment that occupies approximately one-fifth of brain volume, filled with interstitial fluid (ISF), extracellular matrix (ECM), and other secreted molecules, actively involved in the regulation of molecule transportation, cell communications, and signal integration [2 –4].

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: It is therefore important to investigate the structures, functions, and regulation of brain ECS in both normal and pathological conditions, which would facilitate our understanding of the brain homeostasis and disorders.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: Diffusion measurements were used to estimate the volume fraction (⍺ = ECS volume/total brain volume, normally 0.2–0.25) and the tortuosity (λ, normally 1.5–1.6), which indicates the hindrance imposed by brain ECS to molecular diffusion.

claims.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
claims.raw_text: At small scale, interaction of neurons and glia cannot be achieved without the involvement of the diffusion of transmitters and ions in the synaptic cleft via the nearby ECS.

claims.raw_text: At large scale, the extensive communications between ISF in brain ECS and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) are important for the long-distance molecule transport, waste removal and homeostasis of brain microenvironment [13, 14].

evidence.quote: Recent studies have revealed that the brain extracellular space (ECS) is a complex dynamic compartment that occupies approximately one-fifth of brain volume, filled with interstitial fluid (ISF), extracellular matrix (ECM), and other secreted molecules, actively involved in the regulation of molecule transportation, cell communications, and signal integration [2 –4].

evidence.quote: It is therefore important to investigate the structures, functions, and regulation of brain ECS in both normal and pathological conditions, which would facilitate our understanding of the brain homeostasis and disorders.

evidence.quote: These studies suggest that TMS promotes glym phatic Aβ clearance potentially through AQP4 repolarization. Beside the AD model, recent study also reported that rTMS treatment improved glymphatic function and molecule clearance in intracerebral hemorrhage model [255]. Interestingly, continuous theta burst TMS was found to modulate ISF penetration by activating GABAA receptors [256]. Whether TMS directly act on perivascular astrocyte AQP4 via GABA-dependent pathway remains to be explored.

evidence.source_file: C:\Users\25416\Desktop\PKU\outputs\uploads\d446257fddb44854898d74d2d13a43d6\Advancement in modulation of brain extracellular space and unlocking its potential for intervention of neurological diseases.pdf
evidence.quote: It is worth noting that the skull structure made it difficult for red light and near infrared light to penetrate. Although the longer wavelength of light increased the efficiency of transmission, the heating effects should not be ignored [217]. Intermittent stimulation instead of continuous treatment has been applied to reduce thermal effects, leading to positive effect on Aβ clearance [270]. Regardless, delivering sufficient light dose to deeper structures in the human brain with low heating effects continues to be challenging. Ultrasound stimulation A sound wave with a frequency higher than 20 kHz is called ultrasound. Ultrasound has been validated in clinical applications, particularly as an imaging tool for clinical diagnosis. Recently, ultrasound has emerged as a therapeutic tool for local drug or nanoparticle delivery by enlarging the inter stitial spaces within the tissue through non-thermal mechanisms [273–275]. checks.name: Model inference is not mixed into paper claims

detail.policy: paper claims must remain paper_original; generated workflow text is model_infer

steps.action: Dry-run the Paper-to-ARM pipeline on the same input and validate ARM modules.
steps.expected_output: JSON export containing full_arm and trace_record; all nine ARM modules present.
steps.observed_output: ARM modules, source files, claims, evidence and trace prerequisites checked.

metrics.name: Pre-export artifact handling
metrics.expected: artifacts module exists; empty list is allowed before export persistence

limitations.text: This ARM is for research literature structuring only and must not be used for clinical diagnosis, prognosis, prescription, or treatment decisions.
Animal or preclinical findings in source papers cannot be directly transferred to humans without clinical validation.
ECS-related conclusions require manual review because ECS structure, measurement modality, and disease context vary across studies.
Data and code availability are not assumed unless explicitly detected in the source text.

artifacts.description: Complete ARM package and replay trace in JSON.

output.extractor_prompt:
Paper细粒度结论&证据抽取子Agent:
只摘抄原文，禁止自主推导、扩写、脑补数据。每条claim必须包含
claim_id/raw_text/claim_category/source_location/support_evidence_snippet/
conflict_evidence_snippet/source_attribution/ecs_related/evidence_incomplete。
raw_text和support_evidence_snippet必须来自论文原文，不概括、不润色。
无原文直接支撑的猜想、推论丢弃；单篇少于5条有效claim时交由trace标记缺口。
